content:
Builder references a trusted sibling workflow but locally reimplements model logic

Severity: high
Status: confirmed

Canonical side: Builder and metadata language say the offline build is sourced from the trusted sibling modeling workflow.

Current side: The builder performs local feature engineering, model fitting, threshold search, and SHAP generation in this repo rather than importing or executing the sibling script directly.

User-visible risk: Future maintainers may overestimate direct reproducibility against the original workflow and miss local behavioral drift.
title: Builder references a trusted sibling workflow but locally reimplements model logic

content:
employee_scores artifact includes extra columns beyond the declared required contract

Severity: low
Status: confirmed

Canonical side: artifact_contract.json defines a required employee_scores column set and a small optional set.

Current side: Current employee_scores.parquet also contains satisfaction_level, attrition_label, high_risk_flag, and employee_id, which are not declared in the current contract.

User-visible risk: Downstream consumers may make assumptions based on undeclared fields or misread them as guaranteed long-term contract elements.
title: employee_scores artifact includes extra columns beyond the declared required contract

content:
Optional model_modes_summary contract does not match the current file shape

Severity: medium
Status: confirmed

Canonical side: artifact_contract.json declares flattened required fields for model_modes_summary.json.

Current side: The checked-in model_modes_summary.json and builder output use nested keys like operational, survey_rich, comparison_notes, and source_workflow instead of the flattened contract fields.

User-visible risk: Schema validation or future indexing could fail or encode the file incorrectly.
title: Optional model_modes_summary contract does not match the current file shape

content:
Public canonical narrative and current snapshot evidence need explicit separation

Severity: medium
Status: partially_confirmed

Canonical side: Public app copy presents a polished operational story centered on weighted XGBoost, threshold choice, department exposure, and SHAP explainability.

Current side: Current metadata snapshot includes provenance notes and local rerun evidence that are useful for auditability but not always surfaced in the public-facing page copy.

User-visible risk: Future upgrades may either over-audit public copy or over-polish runtime truth and erase the important distinction between narrative and snapshot evidence.
title: Public canonical narrative and current snapshot evidence need explicit separation

content:
Public model selection differs from local rerun metric leader

Severity: high
Status: confirmed

Canonical side: Public operational truth preserves weighted XGBoost at threshold 0.29.

Current side: Current checked-in comparison artifact records rf_balanced as the local rerun best-cost leader at threshold 0.27, while metadata notes still preserve weighted XGBoost at 0.29 as the selected public model.

User-visible risk: Users or future assistants may incorrectly treat the rerun leader table as replacing the published public model story.
title: Public model selection differs from local rerun metric leader

content:
Fallback runtime row logic differs from artifact-backed row truth

Severity: high
Status: confirmed

Canonical side: Artifact-backed row-level truth uses employee_scores.parquet with attrition_probability and selected_threshold_flag.

Current side: Fallback mode derives screening_score_v1, high_risk_flag_v1, and risk_cost_exposure_index_v1 from heuristic feature weighting inside load_data.py.

User-visible risk: If labels blur, a fallback exploratory score could be mistaken for the final model probability.
title: Fallback runtime row logic differs from artifact-backed row truth

content:
Static figure layer can diverge from generated artifact layer

Severity: medium
Status: confirmed

Canonical side: Generated tables and metadata are the current structured runtime-consumed artifact truth when available.

Current side: Several pages still rely on static PNG figures as stable reference or fallback presentation assets without explicit version linkage to the current checked-in artifact snapshot.

User-visible risk: Presentation visuals may lag or differ from the latest structured artifact snapshot without an obvious provenance cue.
title: Static figure layer can diverge from generated artifact layer

content:
Definition: Runtime condition where the app reads checked-in generated V2 artifacts as the main source for metadata, row-level scores, model tables, or explainability outputs.

Preferred usage: Use when describing the normal preferred runtime path in the current repo.

Avoid confusion with:
- fallback mode
- static figure fallback
title: artifact-backed mode

content:
Definition: A deterministic hashed row identity derived from cleaned row content, intended as the stable join key for row-level generated artifacts.

Preferred usage: Use when joining artifact-backed row-level outputs to cleaned data.

Avoid confusion with:
- visible fallback employee_id
- row order
title: employee_id_v2

content:
Definition: Runtime condition where missing V2 artifacts cause parts of the app, especially the Workforce Explorer, to use built-in deterministic heuristics and reference visuals instead of generated row-level outputs.

Preferred usage: Use when describing resilience behavior or explaining why a page is not showing final model outputs.

Avoid confusion with:
- artifact-backed mode
- public model truth
title: fallback mode

content:
Definition: The main public deployment-oriented model and app framing that emphasizes practical, artifact-backed retention screening rather than a survey-heavy or retraining-heavy workflow.

Preferred usage: Use when describing the public model mode, the main app framing, or the preserved public reference selection.

Avoid confusion with:
- survey_rich
- fallback mode
title: operational

content:
Definition: The future organizing method spine for this project: Plan, Analyze, Construct, Execute.

Preferred usage: Use as the structural navigation and governance frame for future navigator, RAG, and orchestration layers.

Avoid confusion with:
- current page tabs
- full agent mode
title: PACE

content:
Definition: The explicit builder rule that preserves the public operational source-of-truth selection of weighted XGBoost at threshold 0.29 even when a local rerun metric leaderboard differs.

Preferred usage: Use when explaining why public canonical truth may differ from a fresh local rerun table.

Avoid confusion with:
- metric leader
- automatic best model selection
title: public reference selection

content:
Definition: An early-warning review workflow for identifying possible attrition exposure that should support human investigation rather than automate employment decisions.

Preferred usage: Use for the app’s business purpose and responsible-use framing.

Avoid confusion with:
- automated HR decision engine
- causal intervention proof
title: retention screening

content:
Definition: A weighted exposure measure used to compare concentration of attrition concern, derived from attrition probability and salary cost weight in artifact-backed mode, or from heuristic screening score and cost weight in fallback mode.

Preferred usage: Use when comparing department exposure or filtered employee burden.

Avoid confusion with:
- attrition_probability
- salary alone
title: risk_cost_exposure_index

content:
Definition: Model interpretation output used here to explain which features contribute most strongly to the chosen model’s distinctions; it is interpretation support, not causal HR truth.

Preferred usage: Use when discussing explainability tables, summary plots, or future drilldowns.

Avoid confusion with:
- causal proof
- policy recommendation
title: SHAP

content:
Definition: An alternate builder mode that keeps stronger explanatory survey-style features such as burnout_index and effort_reward_gap, but is not the main public deployment framing in this repo.

Preferred usage: Use when discussing alternate offline build modes or future comparison studies.

Avoid confusion with:
- operational
title: survey_rich

content:
Definition: The process of sweeping decision thresholds and reviewing precision, recall, F-scores, cost, and flagged counts to choose an operating point for retention screening.

Preferred usage: Use when discussing the threshold curve, confusion matrix, and manager review queue sizing.

Avoid confusion with:
- model retraining
title: threshold tuning

caveats: Contains machine-specific provenance text that should be treated carefully.
content:
Project name: Salifort Motors Retention Risk Explorer
Final model: weighted XGBoost
Selected threshold: 0.29
Model mode: operational
Artifact version: v2-offline-operational
Notes:
- Built outside Streamlit runtime from the trusted exported modeling workflow.
- Source workflow: C:\Users\akbak\OneDrive\Documents\GitHub\salifort-motors-attrition-modeling-python\scripts\02_salifort_motors_capstone_portfolio_project.py
- Threshold selected using the false-negative-heavy cost rule from the source workflow.
- Public operational source-of-truth in the sibling repo README and walkthrough frames the final public version as weighted XGBoost at threshold 0.29.
- Local rerun metric leader was rf_balanced at 0.27.
title: artifacts/v2/metadata.json

caveats: Contains machine-specific provenance text that should be treated carefully.
content:
Operational mode:
- Feature inclusion: Removes direct satisfaction-based modeling features and keeps the more deployment-like public workflow.
- Deployment notes: This is the main public mode used by the Streamlit app and the preferred default for offline artifact generation.

Survey-rich mode:
- Feature inclusion: Keeps stronger explanatory survey-driven features such as burnout_index and effort_reward_gap.
- Deployment notes: Useful for comparison and interpretation, but not the main public deployment framing.

Comparison notes: Both modes come from the trusted sibling modeling workflow. The operational mode is the main public version in this Streamlit repo.
Source workflow: C:\Users\akbak\OneDrive\Documents\GitHub\salifort-motors-attrition-modeling-python\scripts\02_salifort_motors_capstone_portfolio_project.py
title: artifacts/v2/model_modes_summary.json

content:
Runtime policy:
- v1_fallback_supported: True
- streamlit_runtime_generates_model_outputs: False
- streamlit_runtime_retrains_models: False
- streamlit_runtime_infers_metrics_from_images: False

Join strategy:
Primary row key: employee_id_v2
Strategy: deterministic_hash_of_cleaned_row_content

Required artifacts:
- confusion_matrix_at_selected_threshold.csv
- department_exposure.csv
- employee_scores.parquet
- metadata.json
- shap_importance.csv
- threshold_curve.csv
- validation_model_comparison.csv

Optional artifacts:

- model_modes_summary.json
- pr_curve_points.parquet
title: artifacts/v2/schemas/artifact_contract.json

content:
- `metadata.template.json`: starter template for the required metadata artifact
- `schemas/artifact_contract.json`: machine-friendly schema contract for required and optional generated files
title: artifacts/v2/README.md :: Contract files in this directory

content:
The Streamlit runtime requirements were intentionally left unchanged in this repo.

The offline builder depends on the modeling stack used by the trusted source workflow, including:

- `scikit-learn`
- `xgboost`
- `imbalanced-learn`
- `shap`

If those packages are not available locally, the builder exits with a short dependency message instead of generating partial fake outputs.
title: artifacts/v2/README.md :: Dependency note

content:
This directory stores the generated files used by the Streamlit app.

The app should continue to run when these files are absent. Streamlit runtime should only read them when they are present. It should not regenerate model outputs, infer values from images, or retrain models.
title: artifacts/v2/README.md :: Generated Artifact Guide

content:
From the repo root:

```powershell
python scripts/build_v2_artifacts.py
```

Optional flags:

```powershell
python scripts/build_v2_artifacts.py --dry-run
python scripts/build_v2_artifacts.py --skip-shap
python scripts/build_v2_artifacts.py --model-mode operational
python scripts/build_v2_artifacts.py --model-mode survey_rich
```
title: artifacts/v2/README.md :: How to run

content:
The first offline builder for this repo lives at:

- `scripts/build_v2_artifacts.py`

It is designed to run outside Streamlit runtime and uses the trusted sibling modeling workflow as the source-of-truth reference:

- `../salifort-motors-attrition-modeling-python/scripts/02_salifort_motors_capstone_portfolio_project.py`

The builder uses this repo's checked-in CSV at `data/hr_capstone_dataset.csv`, mirrors the cleaned-column conventions already used by the app, and reuses the deterministic `employee_id_v2` row-key strategy from `app/utils/load_data.py`.

For `--model-mode operational`, the builder preserves the public operational source-of-truth framing from the sibling project repo: the published selection is weighted XGBoost at threshold `0.29`. The builder still writes the full rerun validation comparison table so any local metric drift remains visible.
title: artifacts/v2/README.md :: Offline builder

content:
- `employee_shap_sample.parquet`
- `pr_curve_points.parquet`
- `model_modes_summary.json`
title: artifacts/v2/README.md :: Optional artifacts

content:
- `employee_scores.parquet`
- `department_exposure.csv`
- `threshold_curve.csv`
- `validation_model_comparison.csv`
- `confusion_matrix_at_selected_threshold.csv`
- `shap_importance.csv`
- `metadata.json`
title: artifacts/v2/README.md :: Required artifacts

content:
- Fallback behavior remains active when `artifacts/v2/` is empty.
- The app may selectively load generated files when they are available.
- Artifact generation should happen outside Streamlit runtime in an offline modeling or packaging workflow.
title: artifacts/v2/README.md :: Runtime policy

content:
Row-level generated files should use `employee_id_v2` as the primary join key.

`employee_id_v2` is defined in the app loader as a deterministic hash of the cleaned row content using these canonical fields:

- `satisfaction_level`
- `last_evaluation`
- `number_project`
- `average_monthly_hours`
- `tenure`
- `work_accident`
- `left`
- `promotion_last_5years`
- `department`
- `salary`

This avoids relying on row order and is safer for joining `employee_scores.parquet` outputs back into the app.
title: artifacts/v2/README.md :: Stable row-key strategy

content:
When the trusted source workflow and required local ML dependencies are available, the builder can generate:

- `metadata.json`
- `employee_scores.parquet`
- `department_exposure.csv`
- `threshold_curve.csv`
- `validation_model_comparison.csv`
- `confusion_matrix_at_selected_threshold.csv`
- `shap_importance.csv`
- `pr_curve_points.parquet`
- `model_modes_summary.json`

It also attempts `employee_shap_sample.parquet` when SHAP is available.
title: artifacts/v2/README.md :: What it generates

content:
WP2 adds a small internal code layer for consuming the navigator registries introduced in WP1.

Scope of this layer:

- load the registry files with repo-relative paths
- validate them enough for safe programmatic use
- expose deterministic helper/query functions
- support future navigator UI, RAG indexing, and offline orchestration planning

Intentional non-goals in WP2:

- no API integration
- no Streamlit page or routing changes
- no artifact or builder rewrites
- no runtime retraining
- no repo rename

Implementation notes:

- The loader uses only the Python standard library.
- JSON registries are loaded with `json`.
- `navigator/source_registry.yaml` is parsed with a constrained, purpose-built parser for the exact YAML subset used in WP1. This avoids adding a new YAML dependency just to consume one controlled registry file.
- The new code lives under `app/services/` and is not wired into the current app runtime, so current user-facing behavior remains unchanged.

Near-term follow-on use:

- WP3 can import these modules to power a non-invasive navigator page.
- Later retrieval work can index registry content without guessing file semantics.
- Later orchestration work can use the governance summary and drift registry to keep automated workflows aligned with preserved truth rules.
title: docs/navigator/wp2-readonly-consumption-notes.md :: WP2 Read-only Consumption Notes

content:
WP3 adds the first non-invasive Streamlit shell for the future PACE Navigator.

What was added:

- a UI-facing read-model layer under `app/viewmodels/`
- a new page shell at `app/pages/pace_navigator.py`
- conservative navigation wiring so the page is visible in the existing app

Why this is still non-invasive:

- no API integration was introduced
- no runtime artifact logic changed
- no model or builder logic changed
- the page reads only local registry-backed view models
- the page is informational and deterministic, not a chat interface

What remains intentionally deferred:

- action buttons or guided workflows
- topic search input beyond deterministic examples
- retrieval/indexing integration
- API-backed explanations
- Airflow or orchestration integration

What later phases can build on:

- governed routing actions
- richer page recommendations
- source drilldowns
- future retrieval preparation
- future API-assisted navigator behavior once governance boundaries are ready
title: docs/navigator/wp3-page-shell-notes.md :: WP3 Page Shell Notes

content:
WP4 upgrades the PACE Navigator from a static shell into a deterministic governed interaction surface.

What was added:

- controlled topic selection with deterministic routing recommendations
- source-of-truth drilldown for the selected topic
- drift register exploration with severity and status filters
- topic-linked drift highlights

Why this is still non-invasive:

- all interaction is local-only and registry-backed
- no free-text LLM behavior was added
- no API integration was introduced
- no artifact, builder, or model logic changed
- the page remains a governed explainer rather than an execution surface

What remains intentionally deferred:

- direct deep-link navigation actions
- free-text topic understanding
- retrieval preparation and chunking
- embeddings, vector indexing, or semantic search
- API-backed navigator behavior

What later phases can build on:

- governed chunking/index preparation from topic/source/drift views
- richer routing actions or page-launch patterns
- retrieval-backed evidence panels
- future API-assisted navigator workflows that keep truth and drift boundaries intact
title: docs/navigator/wp4-governed-interactions-notes.md :: WP4 Governed Interactions Notes

content:
WP5 adds the first governed retrieval-preparation layer for the Salifort PACE Navigator.

What WP5 produces:

- a deterministic retrieval-pack builder
- a retrieval-pack validator
- a governed eligibility policy
- normalized retrieval documents and structure-aware chunks
- stable provenance back to source paths and registry entities

What this pack is for:

- future API-backed retrieval
- future embedding generation and vector indexing
- future governed answer assembly
- future citation-aware navigator behavior

What this pack is not:

- not a retrieval runtime
- not an embedding index
- not a vector database
- not an API integration
- not a chatbot

Why this is still non-invasive:

- it does not change app behavior
- it does not change modeling or artifact-building logic
- it does not change preserved public truth
- it builds only deterministic local preparation artifacts

What later phases can safely build on:

- embedding generation
- vector indexing
- governed chunk retrieval
- answer assembly with provenance and drift awareness
title: docs/navigator/wp5-retrieval-preparation-notes.md :: WP5 Retrieval Preparation Notes

content:
This folder stores deterministic registry files for the future Salifort PACE Analytics System Navigator.

Work Package 1 keeps these files read-only and out of the current Streamlit runtime path. They are intended to help later phases with source-of-truth resolution, drift explanation, phase mapping, repo governance, and future indexing or orchestration work.

Files in this folder:

- `source_registry.yaml`: inventory of the main sources, artifacts, and presentation layers.
- `truth_registry.json`: canonical truth rules by project layer.
- `drift_register.json`: known or confirmed drift items between layers.
- `pace_phase_map.json`: mapping of the Salifort project into the PACE structure.
- `glossary.json`: normalized definitions for key project terms.
- `repo_rename_assessment.md`: recommendation-only assessment of whether the repo should be renamed later.

Usage guidance for later phases:

- Treat these files as explicit planning inputs, not as runtime configuration.
- Preserve the distinction between public narrative truth, runtime artifact truth, legacy method truth, and fallback heuristic behavior.
- Extend entries by adding new records rather than overwriting prior reasoning without evidence.
title: navigator/README.md :: Navigator Foundation

caveats: Recommendation-only governance note; not runtime or model truth.
content:
Current name: `salifort-retention-risk-explorer-streamlit`

The current name is still accurate for the repo as it exists today. It clearly signals:

- Salifort project scope
- retention-risk focus
- explorer-style app framing
- Streamlit implementation layer

That makes it acceptable for current public portfolio use, current deployment clarity, and GitHub discoverability.
title: navigator/repo_rename_assessment.md :: Current Repo Name Evaluation

caveats: Recommendation-only governance note; not runtime or model truth.
content:
Keeping the current name now reduces short-term friction:

- fewer broken GitHub links
- fewer deployment-name mismatches
- fewer README and badge updates
- less confusion between repo identity and deployed app identity

A later rename would likely require coordinated updates to:

- GitHub remote URLs
- README links
- deployment references
- portfolio references
- any external documentation or pinned links
title: navigator/repo_rename_assessment.md :: Deployment / Streamlit / GitHub Implications

caveats: Recommendation-only governance note; not runtime or model truth.
content:
Do not rename the repo in Work Package 1.

Recommendation for now: keep `salifort-retention-risk-explorer-streamlit` as-is.

Recommendation for later: reassess renaming only after the repo genuinely becomes the broader PACE navigator / governance / orchestration hub. If that threshold is crossed, prefer `salifort-pace-analytics-navigator`.
title: navigator/repo_rename_assessment.md :: Final Recommendation For Now

caveats: Recommendation-only governance note; not runtime or model truth.
content:
- broken inbound GitHub links
- stale clone instructions
- mismatch between repo name and existing deployed app branding
- confusion if rename happens before the broader navigator system is actually present
- possible accidental scope inflation in public messaging
title: navigator/repo_rename_assessment.md :: Migration Risks

caveats: Recommendation-only governance note; not runtime or model truth.
content:
- It matches the actual current repo role: the public Streamlit app layer.
- It stays aligned with the deployed app and current README framing.
- It avoids unnecessary rename churn before navigator, orchestration, or multi-surface capabilities actually exist.
- It preserves continuity for existing links, portfolio references, and Streamlit deployment expectations.
title: navigator/repo_rename_assessment.md :: Pros Of Keeping The Current Name

caveats: Recommendation-only governance note; not runtime or model truth.
content:
For the current public portfolio state, the existing name is still strong because it tells reviewers exactly what they are opening.

If the repo later becomes a wider platform or governance hub, a future rename would improve clarity by signaling that the repo is no longer just the Streamlit explorer.
title: navigator/repo_rename_assessment.md :: Public Portfolio Implications

caveats: Recommendation-only governance note; not runtime or model truth.
content:
A rename may become appropriate later if this repository expands beyond being primarily the Streamlit app layer and becomes the broader system-of-record for:

- PACE navigator registries
- source-of-truth governance
- future RAG indexing
- future agent orchestration
- future Airflow offline orchestration
- possibly multiple app surfaces beyond Streamlit

If that scope expansion happens, the current name may become too implementation-specific and too narrow.
title: navigator/repo_rename_assessment.md :: Reasons To Rename Later

caveats: Recommendation-only governance note; not runtime or model truth.
content:
If the repo later becomes the broader system hub, the strongest future candidate is:

1. `salifort-pace-analytics-navigator`

Additional acceptable candidates:

1. `salifort-pace-analytics-system`
2. `salifort-pace-system-navigator`

Why `salifort-pace-analytics-navigator` is the best candidate:

- it preserves Salifort project identity
- it reflects the future PACE method spine
- it is broader than Streamlit but not prematurely agent-branded
- it remains suitable for portfolio, GitHub, and system-navigation use
title: navigator/repo_rename_assessment.md :: Recommended Future Name Candidates

content: https://salifort-retention-risk-explorer.streamlit.app/

```bash
streamlit run app/app.py
```

content:
- `data/hr_capstone_dataset.csv`

title: README.md :: Main files

content: This is an educational project, not a live HR system. It is meant to support review and discussion, not automate employment decisions. Model training and artifact generation happen outside Streamlit runtime, and the app reads generated outputs when they are available.
title: README.md :: Notes and limitations

content: How can Salifort Motors identify attrition exposure early enough to support targeted, responsible retention action without turning the workflow into an automated HR decision engine?
title: README.md :: Project question

content: Streamlit app for the Salifort Motors retention risk project.
title: README.md :: Salifort Retention Risk Explorer (Streamlit)

content:
This app presents the project as a lightweight decision-support tool for reviewing attrition risk.

Main themes:
- threshold-based retention screening
- workforce exploration
- workload and tenure risk patterns
- threshold trade-offs
- SHAP-based explainability
- department exposure and manager action view
title: README.md :: What this app shows

content:
This repository contains the public app layer for the Salifort Motors project. It uses the checked-in HR dataset, generated model tables, and existing project visuals to present an operational decision-support view of attrition risk.

The Workforce Explorer supports interactive review of employee and department patterns. Model comparison, threshold analysis, department exposure, and explainability are powered by generated artifacts built outside Streamlit runtime.
title: README.md :: What this repo contains

caveats: Old repo method links are external legacy references when they point outside this repo.
content:
Analyze

Goal: Explain workforce patterns, EDA structure, and row-level or department-level exploratory signals that motivate intervention review.

Portfolio meaning: This phase captures the evidence and pattern-reading layer behind retention screening, including workload, tenure, department, and salary context.
title: Analyze

caveats: Old repo method links are external legacy references when they point outside this repo.
content:
Construct

Goal: Represent model building, artifact generation, threshold selection logic, and explainability generation as governed build outputs.

Portfolio meaning: This phase captures the modeling and artifact-construction spine behind the public story, while acknowledging that current repo behavior mixes declared provenance with local builder implementation.
title: Construct

caveats: Old repo method links are external legacy references when they point outside this repo.
content:
Execute

Goal: Present the operational decision-support workflow that managers or reviewers would use to inspect exposure, threshold tradeoffs, and explainability outputs.

Portfolio meaning: This phase is the current app’s strongest realized layer: it turns cleaned data and generated artifacts into a deployable review interface while preserving responsible-use messaging.
title: Execute

caveats: Old repo method links are external legacy references when they point outside this repo.
content:
Plan

Goal: Frame the business question, decision boundary, governance limits, and public operational identity of the project.

Portfolio meaning: This phase answers why the project exists, what responsible retention screening means, and why the repo is positioned as a decision-support app rather than an automated HR engine.
title: Plan

content:
Fallback heuristic is exploratory support, not final model probability

Description: The built-in fallback explorer logic derives a screening score and related flags for interactive review when row-level artifacts are absent. It must remain clearly separated from the final weighted XGBoost probability layer.

Authority rule: If fallback mode remains in future upgrades, preserve explicit labeling that the fallback score is not the final model output.

Preserve in upgrade: yes
title: Fallback heuristic is exploratory support, not final model probability

content:
PACE is the method spine for interpreting the project lifecycle

Description: The Salifort project should be organized and later navigated through the PACE structure: Plan, Analyze, Construct, Execute. Legacy modeling workflow context may sit behind parts of Analyze and Construct, while the current app emphasizes Execute.

Authority rule: Use PACE as the organizing frame for future navigator routing, source grouping, and drift explanation even when the current app pages are not yet explicitly labeled by phase.

Preserve in upgrade: yes
title: PACE is the method spine for interpreting the project lifecycle

content:
Future orchestration should remain offline-first and source-governed

Description: Future navigator, agent, RAG, or Airflow layers should treat the current app and artifact bundle as governed inputs. They should explain source-of-truth and drift rather than replacing existing runtime contracts by default.

Authority rule: Any future orchestration layer should preserve offline build separation, source registries, and explicit drift tracking before adding agentic automation.

Preserve in upgrade: yes
title: Future orchestration should remain offline-first and source-governed

content:
Project identity remains the public app-layer operational presentation

Description: The current repository is the newer Streamlit app layer for the Salifort project, intended to present a practical, deployment-friendly, artifact-backed operational decision-support workflow rather than the original notebook-first modeling workspace.

Authority rule: If repo identity language drifts, preserve the README plus current page framing that this repo is the public app layer and not the legacy modeling workspace.

Preserve in upgrade: yes
title: Project identity remains the public app-layer operational presentation

content:
Weighted XGBoost at threshold 0.29 remains the public operational model truth

Description: For the public operational story presented by this repo, the final model truth remains weighted XGBoost with selected threshold 0.29, even though local rerun comparison artifacts also record a different metric leader.

Authority rule: When local rerun leaderboards differ from the published public selection, preserve the explicitly declared public reference selection unless a later governed change intentionally replaces it.

Preserve in upgrade: yes
title: Weighted XGBoost at threshold 0.29 remains the public operational model truth

content:
App runtime is artifact-backed and offline-build oriented

Description: The app runtime should read generated artifacts when available, fall back when necessary, and avoid retraining models or regenerating final analytics inside Streamlit runtime.

Authority rule: Preserve the separation between runtime consumption and offline generation. New features may inspect artifacts but should not silently move model fitting into Streamlit runtime.

Preserve in upgrade: yes
title: App runtime is artifact-backed and offline-build oriented
